text.title: growth-seo-link-building Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for growth-seo-link-building. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Create content that journalists and bloggers need but that doesn't yet exist or is outdated — this is the only reliably scalable link magnet formula.
rule.rationale: Generic content earns generic results. Link magnets are assets people reference repeatedly: original data, free tools, comprehensive guides, industry benchmarks. Without a genuinely link-worthy asset, outreach volume cannot compensate.

rule.statement: Build a prospect list of 100+ sites before starting outreach — quality and volume both matter.
rule.rationale: Response rates average 10-20%; link acquisition rate averages 5-10%. Starting with fewer than 100 prospects produces statistically insignificant results and cannot reveal which tactics work.

rule.statement: Personalize every outreach email — generic emails are ignored at rates above 90%.
rule.rationale: Outreach recipients receive dozens of generic link requests daily. A personalized first line referencing specific content they published signals genuine effort and increases response rates from ~2% (generic) to ~15-20% (personalized).

rule.statement: Follow up exactly once, 7 days after the initial send — never follow up more than once.
rule.rationale: A single follow-up recovers responses from busy recipients who missed the first email. Multiple follow-ups damage relationship and sender reputation without proportional benefit.

rule.statement: Audit internal links monthly: link new posts to relevant old content, update old posts to link to new content, build pillar-cluster structures with keyword-rich anchors.
rule.rationale: Internal links are the highest-ROI link building tactic — zero outreach effort, immediate implementation, direct PageRank distribution, and visible ranking improvements within weeks not months.
